The '''mythic past''' (also called '''[[Mythical|mythical past]]''') in Aelaki refers to events so distant in time that only legends and oral traditions remain. It is used for creation stories, mythological narratives, and events from the deep past.

== Formation ==

The mythic past is marked by the suffix '''-sher''', which is applied to the verb stem without any additional evidential markers.

== Diachronic notes ==

The suffix '''-sher''' is historically a fusion of two [[Past|past tense]] evidentials:

* '''-shë''' (visual/witnessed) + '''-ro''' (auditory/hearsay)

This etymology reflects the nature of mythic knowledge: events that were once witnessed ('''-shë''') but are now known only through oral transmission ('''-ro'''). The fusion into a single morpheme '''-sher''' mirrors how the boundary between direct observation and retelling dissolves over deep time.

== See also ==

* [[Past]]
* [[Hesternal]]
* [[Present]]
* [[Future]]
* [[Hodiernal]]
* [[Crastinal]]

[[Category:grammatical tenses]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
